prompts.subject: a First Age Noldorin heavy warrior of Hithlum, host of Fingolfin
prompts.caption: Noldor of Hithlum
prompts.prompt: Subject: a First Age Noldorin heavy warrior of Hithlum, host of Fingolfin. Lore: tall grey-eyed Elf in a long hauberk of bright Noldorin ring-mail, a high crested helm, a tall oval shield and a long straight sword and spear; banners of blue and silver; cold northern war-gear, proud bearing. A hand-drawn film-workshop costume design plate, traditional pen-and-ink with watercolour and marker washes on warm off-white cream paper, in the manner of a physical concept sketch scanned from an artist's sketchbook. A SINGLE figure, full body head to feet, standing in a relaxed three-quarter view, centred on an otherwise empty warm-white page with only a faint sketchy pencil ground-shadow and a wisp of grass beneath the feet — NO scenery, NO framing. Drawn with thin, confident dark sepia-brown pen linework and DENSE hand-inked cross-hatching for every shadow; the surface detail is obsessive and hand-drawn — each ring of the chainmail, each overlapping scale, each rivet, buckle, stitch, strap, engraved knot and cloth fold rendered individually with fine ink strokes. Colour is laid on like real watercolour and felt-tip markers: soft, muted, slightly desaturated tones that streak, pool and mottle unevenly across each area — never a flat digital fill; visible paper texture shows through the washes. Muted earthy palette — aged golds and brass rendered as cream and ochre with brown shadow-hatching and a few tiny bright-white highlight flecks, olive and teal greens, oxblood and faded scarlet, slate greys, worn leather browns. Cel-style shading kept simple — one mid-tone, darker cross-hatched shadows, a few pale highlights — so forms stay readable without heavy blending. Proportions realistic but subtly elongated and elegant, anatomy correct and believable, every weapon and piece of armour gripped, buckled and worn exactly as it really would be. A short handwritten flowing calligraphic ink caption sits near the bottom of the page. Looks like traditional media on paper — NOT vector art, NOT cel-shaded cartoon, NOT comic-book, NOT anime, NOT a smooth digital painting, NO gradients, NO glow, NO thick uniform outlines. The calligraphic caption reads "Noldor of Hithlum".
